# Adam Zachary Wasserman

> Author and researcher: philosophy of science, artificial intelligence, and the metaphysics of complex emergent structure.

## Who is Adam Zachary Wasserman

Adam Zachary Wasserman is an author and independent researcher working at the intersection of philosophy of science, artificial intelligence, and the metaphysics of complex emergent structure. The unifying through-line across the writing since 2022 is the principle that distributed multi-agent collaboration across substrates and time is the better explanation for complex emergent structure than either reductionist randomness or singular design.

The research is grounded in a pre-registered program of controlled cross-linguistic experiments on large language model training dynamics. Forty years of prior enterprise-technology practice (CTO at IATA, the International Air Transport Association; Director of Consulting at CGI; senior roles at Bombardier and Yellow Pages) preceded a deliberate 2017 pivot toward contributing to the body of knowledge. He has no formal degree. The work is the credential.

ORCID: 0009-0002-8865-6583

## Current research program

A current research program built on a foundational point of view: every scientific instrument has limits, and the discipline of asking what each instrument can and cannot adjudicate is the move that everything downstream depends on. *The Robot in the Dark* sets out the point of view itself. The pre-registered cross-linguistic scientific work listed below is its empirical application. The *Honest Code* book and the Open Honest framework and audit apply it to enterprise software. *The Replicators* is the metaphysical synthesis that arises when the foundation, the empirical results, and a particular philosophical structure are taken together: a parsimonious account of the origin of complex emergent structure in which many collaborative intelligences operate across substrates and time.

- **The Robot in the Dark: Instruments, Illusions, and the Limits of Knowledge** (foundational; manuscript in preparation): Every scientific instrument has an edge where the light runs out. The most consequential moments in science are not what happens inside the illuminated region but what happens at the boundary. Case studies in evolutionary biology, large language model training, software engineering, and cognitive psychology. The book argues that instruments which name their edges become more authoritative, not less.

- **Pre-registered scientific work** (empirical application): A pre-registered program of controlled experiments on cross-linguistic large language model training dynamics. Four deposited preprints; child-scale extension accepted for presentation at EMNLP 2026 Budapest. Detailed below.

- **Honest Code and Open Honest** (operational application): The same point of view applied to enterprise software. *Honest Code* (commercially published) presents the principles; the Honest framework is the correct-by-construction development framework whose limits are bounded by architecture rather than by hope; the Honest audit is a peer-reviewable measurement instrument whose own thresholds are explicit and falsifiable. Eight pre-registered studies (Papers A through H) sit alongside the working code. Detailed below.

- **The Replicators: Multiple Collaborative Intelligences as the Substrate of Complex Structure** (metaphysical synthesis; in preparation): Complex emergent structure (biological evolution, software systems, language itself) is more parsimoniously explained by many collaborative intelligences operating across substrates and time than by either single-creator or no-creator accounts. The argument arises out of three things taken together: Robot's foundational point of view, the empirical results from the cross-linguistic scientific work, and a particular philosophical structure that includes the convergence-of-signals method (multiple independent instruments meeting at the same boundary), the discount-bias diagnosis (in which our use of consciousness as the measuring instrument leads us to discount any agency that does not look like human consciousness), and the silicon-vs-DNA parsimony argument (if binary silicon transformers can plausibly host intelligence, then DNA codons with 64 possible permutations doubly so).

## Books

- **The Robot in the Dark** (in preparation, 2026): Manuscript draft; Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20382928 (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20382928).
- **The Replicators** (in preparation): Outline complete; sample chapters drawn from the Weinstein vs. Dawkins three-part Substack series (2022).
- [Honest Code](https://honestcode.software): Sixteen principles for eliminating hidden state in software. Side-by-side comparisons in twelve programming languages with real nanosecond execution traces.
- [The Chaos Factory](https://amzn.to/46I8piO) (2017): The inside story of corporate IT failure. [Favorably reviewed by Bloor Research](https://www.bloorresearch.com/the-chaos-factory-by-adam-wasserman/).

## Pre-registered empirical research

Pre-registered program on cross-linguistic LLM training dynamics. Collaborators on BabyLM 2026 and the EMNLP main-conference extension: David Beauchemin (Laval University) and Hafedh Mili (Universit\u00e9 du Qu\u00e9bec \u00e0 Montr\u00e9al).

- [The Scaling Hypothesis Is Language-Contingent: Evidence from Cross-Linguistic Training Dynamics](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19423151) (Wasserman 2026; Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19423151; OSF pre-registration https://osf.io/sj48b): Pre-registered controlled ablation training identical 125M-parameter transformers on matched English and French C4 corpora. French achieves grammatical competence at 197M tokens; English remains at chance through 4+ billion tokens.
- [English Considered Harmful](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19443358) (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19443358): Companion preprint to the language-contingent scaling line.
- [The 70% Rule: When Axiomatic Prompting Helps, and When It Hurts](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19423101) (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19423101; OSF pre-registrations https://osf.io/7z49a and https://osf.io/mzf79).
- [Process Discipline](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19355460) (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19355460): Methodological preprint on pre-registered empirical practice.

## Writing

- [Essential Musings](https://emusings.substack.com): Substack archive, 40+ essays since 2022. Includes the foundational *Weinstein vs. Dawkins* three-part series (May 2022) introducing the Replicators thesis, *The Case That AI Is Thinking* (Nov 2025), *Rovelli Says Reality Is Relative \u2014 But His Math Needs a God* (Oct 2025), *AI and the Consciousness Gap* (May 2022), and *The Robot in the Dark: Pass 1 \u2014 The Retreat* (Jan 2026).
- [Hackernoon](https://hackernoon.com/u/azw): Technology articles.
- [Welcome to the Jungle](https://www.welcometothejungle.com/en/authors/adam-zachary-wasserman): Articles on enterprise technology and careers.
- [Hacker News](https://news.ycombinator.com/user?id=adamzwasserman): Active contributor with substantive historical and conceptual commentary.

## Open source

### DATAOS ecosystem

DATAOS (DOM As The Authority On State) is the operational philosophy underlying *Honest Code*. Its premise is that the DOM, treated as the single authoritative source of application state, eliminates the need for state-management libraries entirely.

- [DATAOS](https://dataos.software): Architecture overview and ecosystem hub.
- [domx](https://domx.software): DOM state observer library, under 1KB, zero dependencies.
- [genX](https://genx.software): Declarative behavior via HTML attributes, under 1KB.
- [stateless](https://stateless.software): React hook that reads state from DOM instead of useState.

### Open Honest

Open Honest is the empirical and operational research program built around the Honest Code principles. Eight pre-registered studies (Papers A through H) sit alongside the working code.

- [Honest framework](https://honestframework.software): Correct-by-construction development framework with multi-language analyzer, four-language tree-sitter detector, and a 276-scenario behavioural specification. Architectural constraints bound the codebase to known behaviour domains.
- **Honest audit**: Four-layer measurement instrument for enterprise software quality. Published with explicit indicator thresholds and a peer-reviewable behavioural specification of its own behaviour at boundary cases.

## Other professional work

- **Buckler AI**: AI compliance for regulated markets. Developed the ClearSignal methodology for compliance attestation. Fractional CTO.
- **Sizzl**: Fractional CTO consultancy for enterprise technology.
- [multicardz](https://www.multicardz.com): Next-generation kanban. Add rows, change the criteria used to build rows and columns on demand, and handle sets of over 1 million cards in under 1 second. ~140K lines of production code across 17 subsystems.

## Key concepts associated with Adam Zachary Wasserman

Multi-collaborative intelligences, the Language-Only Hypothesis, instrument-aware epistemology, the convergence-of-signals method (multiple independent instruments meeting at the same boundary), the discount-bias diagnosis (consciousness as measuring instrument biases us against non-human-shaped agency), the silicon-vs-DNA parsimony argument, language-contingent scaling, M\u00c9TRON measurement-instrument framing, Honest Code, DATAOS (DOM As The Authority On State), pure functions over methods, TypedDict over classes, I/O at the boundary.
